flashcards.definition: A = ½ab sin C, where a and b are two sides and C is the included angle.
For a regular n-gon inscribed in a circle of radius r: side = 2r sin(π/n).
A = ½nr² sin(2π/n) for a regular n-gon inscribed in a circle of radius r.
d = √(l² + w²); the angle with the base can be found using tan θ = w/l.
A = ½r²θ (θ in radians); links radian measure to geometric area.
